title: Molecular-data availability index (accession + access-tier label)
context: An open metadata catalog must never imply open access to a controlled accession; this index labels each molecular entry's access tier and links out only, re-hosting no payloads (PLAN M2). Depends on the v0.1 release (011). Binding cancer guardrails (PLAN section 7): open-access/aggregate/de-identified metadata only; no controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA) or patient-identifiable data; COSMIC/OncoKB reference-only (not ingested); provenance on every assertion; links only, no omics/sequence/image payloads re-hosted; no medical advice. No partner/requestor secured yet, so verifiedNeed is false.
objective: Build a molecular-data availability index where each entry records {assay, accession, repository, accessTier, license} with an explicit access-tier label and link-only references.
acceptanceCriteria: Each molecular entry = {assay, accession, repository, accessTier, license}; access tier labeled so an open catalog never implies open access to a controlled accession.
Links only; no sequence/matrix/image payloads re-hosted; dead-link checked.
output: output/ molecular-availability index dataset with per-entry access-tier labels and link-only references.
